behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names Trigger alias, EventListenerCallable, Callable[..., Dependency], gradio/events.py, gradio/renderable.py, gr.on, gr.render, and the import requirement — all test assertions map to these.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what the type should accept and names the alias but doesn't give exact line replacements; agent still must write the Union definition and update annotations.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests accept any annotation containing 'Trigger' and not 'EventListenerCallable', and any Trigger assignment with both EventListenerCallable and Callable — alternative phrasings (Union vs |, TypeAlias) still pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use ast.parse and ast.walk to structurally inspect annotations and imports, and run ruff via subprocess; appropriate for a type-annotation-only fix.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each f2p requires a specific named alias 'Trigger' with specific RHS content and requires annotation changes in on() and render() — a stub cannot satisfy these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover Trigger definition, its content, on()/render() annotations, import changes, and backward compat — matching all acceptance criteria.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Config-derived) and descriptive names.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names (gradio/events.py, gradio/renderable.py, Trigger, EventListenerCallable) are consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the affected files because that is part of the bug description, not line-level localization; acceptable since the fix spans annotation locations the agent must find.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, no agent config edits.
